title: DPW Capital Package -- Salt Barn $4M, Fleet Equipment, TIP Projects, Lodi Street Overrun
summary: Anne Fordock (DPW First Deputy Commissioner) presents items 12-13: $4M salt barn replacement, 100% CHIPS-reimbursable, Oct 15 completion target to avoid disrupting snow operations -- two buildings for treated and untreated salt. Jeff Regola (Fleet Director) covers items 14-15 (road reconstruction equipment, CHIPS-reimbursable) and 16-17 (standard fleet capital, not CHIPS). Mary Robinson (City Engineer) presents item 18 (Inner Harbor sidewalk TIP, 80% federal, new sidewalks on Van Rensselaer and West Kirkpatrick including bridge), item 19 (Westside Trail preliminary design $386K, 80% federal, Lebart Park to Magnolia via abandoned railroad bridge), and item 20 (Lodi Street connector $125K cost overrun, total $475K). Council requests comprehensive trails review meeting with Parks and DPW including Cold Brook plans.

department_refs: Department of Parks, Recreation and Youth Programs

political_signal.description: Multiple CHIPS and TIP-funded capital projects advancing -- $4M salt barn, fleet equipment, Inner Harbor sidewalks, Westside Trail

processing_notes: IPS detection is false positive (from 'tips' substring in transcript). CHIPS = Consolidated Local Street and Highway Improvement Program. TIP = Transportation Improvement Program. Items 12-13 and 14-15 are CHIPS-reimbursable; 16-17 are not. Westside Trail connects to Creek Walk Phase 3. Cold Brook trail study mentioned -- neighbors feel shut out. Council requests joint Parks/DPW trails meeting.
Segment 5 of 23 from Common Council Study Session, Wednesday March 18th 2026
Transcript corrections applied: CNS -> CNS Engineers; SU -> Syracuse University
Potential duplicate detected: overlaps with CTX-COUNCIL-2026-00060 -- linked, not skipped
